steps.title: Recall what y-intercept means
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis. This occurs when $x = 0$. We need to find the coordinates of this crossing point.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Locating where the line crosses the y-axis at $x = 0$, the line intersects the y-axis at the point $(0, -4)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
